revision.comment: 新しいページ：科野大宮社。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 科野大宮社 |画像 = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
拝殿とケヤキ |所在地 = [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=科野大宮社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[事代主|事代主命]] |神体 = |社格 = （推定）[[信濃国]][[総社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''科野大宮社'''（しなのおおみやしゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[信濃国]][[総社]]と推定され、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[大国主|大己貴命]]''' （おおなむちのみこと）祭神は『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項による。 * '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[建御名方神|健御名方富命]] （たけみなかたとみのみこと） == 歴史 == 信濃国府は古来上田市にあったとされるが、平安時代初期に松本市へ移されたとされる『長野県の地名』信濃国節。。上田市の国府は神科台地上と推定されており、当社は国司が参拝する[[総社]]であったとする説がある。なお、付近には[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]]・[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]]も残っている。 当社は古来「総社大宮」「科野国魂神」と称したといわれ、中世には「大宮諏訪大明神」と称したと伝える『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項。。 [[真田氏]]による[[上田城]]築城以来、当社は城の鎮守と定められ、[[信濃国分寺]]三重塔と当社は藩費をもって修繕したという。[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）頃の城下町古地図には「常田村大宮」と記載されている。 [[明治]]に入り、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[県社]]に列し、社名を現在の「科野大宮社」に改めた。 == 境内 == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|社叢（市指定天然記念物）]] 境内には大木が何本も立っており、社叢は市指定天然記念物に指定されている。 現在の社殿は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の上田城主・[[松平忠礼]]の再建によるもの。境内社の六所明神と駒形稲荷社は、[[天正]]年間の築城の際に上田旧城地から当地に移されたと伝える。 File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|本殿（右）と稲荷社（左） == 文化財 == === 上田市指定文化財 === * 科野大宮社社叢 - 昭和44年5月9日指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 ; 交通アクセス * [[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）・[[しなの鉄道]]・[[上田電鉄]] [[上田駅]] （徒歩約12分） ; 周辺 * [[上田城]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺]] - 現在の後継寺院。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上田市 科野大宮社項 == 関連項目 == * [[伊和神社 (松本市)|伊和神社]] （長野県松本市） - 当社と同じく信濃国総社と推定される。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html 科野大宮社社叢]（上田市文化財マップ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなのおおみやしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:上田市]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:信濃国|社00]]

{{神社 |名称 = 科野大宮社 |画像 = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
拝殿とケヤキ |所在地 = [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=科野大宮社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[事代主|事代主命]] |神体 = |社格 = （推定）[[信濃国]][[総社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）第10代[[崇神天皇]]年間 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''科野大宮社'''（しなのおおみやしゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[信濃国]][[総社]]と推定され、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[大国主|大己貴命]]''' （おおなむちのみこと）祭神は『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項による。 * '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[建御名方神|健御名方富命]] （たけみなかたとみのみこと） == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、第10代[[崇神天皇]]の御世に[[神八井耳命]]の孫・[[建五百建命]]（たけいほたけのみこと）による創建という『日本の神々』科野大宮項。。 [[天武天皇]]の御世に神地の下賜があり、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]]-[[749年]]）には官祭執行の儀があったともいう。 信濃国府は古来上田市にあったとされるが、平安時代初期に松本市へ移されたとされる『長野県の地名』信濃国節。。上田市の国府は神科台地上と推定されており、当社は国司が参拝する[[総社]]であったとする説がある。なお、付近には[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]]・[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]]も残っている。 当社は古来「総社大宮」「科野国魂神」と称したといわれ、中世には「大宮諏訪大明神」と称したと伝える『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項。。 [[真田氏]]による[[上田城]]築城以来、当社は城の鎮守と定められ、[[信濃国分寺]]三重塔と当社は藩費をもって修繕したという。[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）頃の城下町古地図には「常田村大宮」と記載されている。 [[明治]]に入り、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[県社]]に列し、社名を現在の「科野大宮社」に改めた。 == 境内 == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|社叢（市指定天然記念物）]] 境内には大木が何本も立っており、社叢は市指定天然記念物に指定されている。 現在の社殿は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の上田城主・[[松平忠礼]]の再建によるもの。境内社の六所明神と駒形稲荷社は、[[天正]]年間の築城の際に上田旧城地から当地に移されたと伝える。 File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|本殿（右）と稲荷社（左） == 文化財 == === 上田市指定文化財 === * 科野大宮社社叢 - 昭和44年5月9日指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 ; 交通アクセス * [[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）・[[しなの鉄道]]・[[上田電鉄]] [[上田駅]] （徒歩約12分） ; 周辺 * [[上田城]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺]] - 現在の後継寺院。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上田市 科野大宮社項 * 黒坂周平・滝澤主税「科野大宮」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） == 関連項目 == * [[伊和神社 (松本市)|伊和神社]] （長野県松本市） - 当社と同じく信濃国総社と推定される。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html 科野大宮社社叢]（上田市文化財マップ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなのおおみやしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:上田市]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:信濃国|社00]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）第10代[[崇神天皇]]年間 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''科野大宮社'''（しなのおおみやしゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[信濃国]][[総社]]と推定され、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[大国主|大己貴命]]''' （おおなむちのみこと）祭神は『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項による。 * '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[建御名方神|健御名方富命]] （たけみなかたとみのみこと） == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、第10代[[崇神天皇]]の御世に[[神八井耳命]]の孫・[[健磐龍命|建五百建命]]（たけいほたけのみこと）による創建という『日本の神々』科野大宮項。。 [[天武天皇]]の御世に神地の下賜があり、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]]-[[749年]]）には官祭執行の儀があったともいう。 信濃国府は古来上田市にあったとされるが、平安時代初期に松本市へ移されたとされる『長野県の地名』信濃国節。。上田市の国府は神科台地上と推定されており、当社は国司が参拝する[[総社]]であったとする説がある。なお、付近には[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]]・[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]]も残っている。 当社は古来「総社大宮」「科野国魂神」と称したといわれ、中世には「大宮諏訪大明神」と称したと伝える『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項。。 [[真田氏]]による[[上田城]]築城以来、当社は城の鎮守と定められ、[[信濃国分寺]]三重塔と当社は藩費をもって修繕したという。[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）頃の城下町古地図には「常田村大宮」と記載されている。 [[明治]]に入り、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[県社]]に列し、社名を現在の「科野大宮社」に改めた。 == 境内 == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|社叢（市指定天然記念物）]] 境内には大木が何本も立っており、社叢は市指定天然記念物に指定されている。 現在の社殿は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の上田城主・[[松平忠礼]]の再建によるもの。境内社の六所明神と駒形稲荷社は、[[天正]]年間の築城の際に上田旧城地から当地に移されたと伝える。 File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|本殿（右）と稲荷社（左） == 文化財 == === 上田市指定文化財 === * 科野大宮社社叢 - 昭和44年5月9日指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 ; 交通アクセス * [[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）・[[しなの鉄道]]・[[上田電鉄]] [[上田駅]] （徒歩約12分） ; 周辺 * [[上田城]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺]] - 現在の後継寺院。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上田市 科野大宮社項 * 黒坂周平・滝澤主税「科野大宮」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） == 関連項目 == * [[伊和神社 (松本市)|伊和神社]] （長野県松本市） - 当社と同じく信濃国総社と推定される。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html 科野大宮社社叢]（上田市文化財マップ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなのおおみやしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:信濃国|社00]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

所在地 * [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 ; 交通アクセス * [[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）・[[しなの鉄道]]・[[上田電鉄]] [[上田駅]] （徒歩約12分） ; 周辺 * [[上田城]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺]] - 現在の後継寺院。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上田市 科野大宮社項 * 黒坂周平・滝澤主税「科野大宮」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） == 関連項目 == * [[伊和神社 (松本市)|伊和神社]] （長野県松本市） - 当社と同じく信濃国総社と推定される。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html 科野大宮社社叢]（上田市文化財マップ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなのおおみやしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:信濃国|社00]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）第10代[[崇神天皇]]年間 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''科野大宮社'''（しなのおおみやしゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[信濃国]][[総社]]と推定され、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[大国主|大己貴命]]''' （おおなむちのみこと）祭神は『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項による。 * '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[建御名方神|健御名方富命]] （たけみなかたとみのみこと） == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、第10代[[崇神天皇]]の御世に[[神八井耳命]]の孫・[[武五百建命|建五百建命]]（たけいほたけのみこと）による創建という『日本の神々』科野大宮項。。 [[天武天皇]]の御世に神地の下賜があり、[[天平]]年間（[[729年]]-[[749年]]）には官祭執行の儀があったともいう。 信濃国府は古来上田市にあったとされるが、平安時代初期に松本市へ移されたとされる『長野県の地名』信濃国節。。上田市の国府は神科台地上と推定されており、当社は国司が参拝する[[総社]]であったとする説がある。なお、付近には[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]]・[[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]]も残っている。 当社は古来「総社大宮」「科野国魂神」と称したといわれ、中世には[[常田荘]]の中心に位置し、「大宮諏訪大明神」と称したと伝える『長野県の地名』科野大宮社項。。[[康安]]2年（1362年）には[[関東管領]][[足利基氏]]が、彗星出現につき天下安全を当社に祈り、願文を奉ったとされる『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』p.759 角川書店、1990年。 [[真田氏]]による[[上田城]]築城以来、当社は城の鎮守と定められ、[[信濃国分寺]]三重塔と当社は藩費をもって修繕したという。[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）頃の城下町古地図には「常田村大宮」と記載されている。 [[明治]]に入り、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[県社]]に列し、社名を現在の「科野大宮社」に改めた。 == 境内 == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|社叢（市指定天然記念物）]] 境内には大木が何本も立っており、社叢は市指定天然記念物に指定されている。 現在の社殿は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の上田城主・[[松平忠礼]]の再建によるもの。境内社の六所明神と駒形稲荷社は、[[天正]]年間の築城の際に上田旧城地から当地に移されたと伝える。 File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|本殿（右）と稲荷社（左） == 文化財 == === 上田市指定文化財 === * 科野大宮社社叢 - 昭和44年5月9日指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長野県]][[上田市]]常入字上常田723-1 ; 交通アクセス * [[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）・[[しなの鉄道]]・[[上田電鉄]] [[上田駅]] （徒歩約12分） ; 周辺 * [[上田城]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分寺跡|信濃国分寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺#信濃国分尼寺跡|信濃国分尼寺跡]] * [[信濃国分寺]] - 現在の後継寺院。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上田市 科野大宮社項 * 黒坂周平・滝澤主税「科野大宮」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） == 関連項目 == * [[伊和神社 (松本市)|伊和神社]] （長野県松本市） - 当社と同じく信濃国総社と推定される。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html 科野大宮社社叢]（上田市文化財マップ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなのおおみやしや}} [[Category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:信濃国|社00]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | Image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | Address = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | Founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_Names = | Festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaiimimi no Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place Names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place Names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === Location === ; Address * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property Map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === Location === ; Address * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property Map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === Location === ; Address * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property Map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Check_everybodywiki}}[[Category:original pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | Address = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | Founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_Names = | Festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place Names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place Names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === Location === ; Address * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property Map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Check_everybodywiki}}[[Category:original pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. {{Shrine | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | Address = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | Founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | Festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === Location === ; Address * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property Map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] to [[Category:Ken-sha]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[Category:dd]][[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[Category:dd]][[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[Category:dd]][[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine
