revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja revision.comment: Adding [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) === Cultural Properties === ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * **Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove** – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. === location === ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] === Deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." === Grounds === [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanori|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Shinto shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Ueda, Nagano]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shinano Omiya Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:科野大宮社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}}. [[ja:科野大宮社]] == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, by {{ill|Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en}}, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|ja|松平忠礼}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en}}.
