{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]] [[Category:上田市の歴史]]

{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[JR East]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[Shinano Railway]], or [[Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha haiden-2.JPG|280px]]
Haiden and Zelkova tree | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|40.04|N|138|15|28.80|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinano Ōmiya Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|Emperor Tenmu|" to "|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|qid=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|qid=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}}

During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] [[sn:hinano Ōmiyasha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[sn:Shinano Ōmiyasha]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|qid=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}}

The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
