revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228733]] Wikidata name: Shinano-omiya-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by [[:en:Matsudaira Tadanari|Matsudaira Tadanari]], the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Shinano Railway|Shinano Railway]], or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]]. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. [[co:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[co:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari|1=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[co:commons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shinano-omiya-sha]][[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Shinano Ōmiya Shrine | image = File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG | location = Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.394456|N|138.258|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852|1=Ueda, Nagano}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi|1=Kamuyaimimi}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era|1=Tenpyō}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari|1=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (Tradition) During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|October 15|10月15日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|WD=Q844852|1=Ueda, Nagano}} }} [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|科野大宮社}}, ''Shinano Ōmiya-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|上田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Main deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Kotoshironushi no Mikoto) ; Associated deity * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded during the reign of the 10th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, by {{ill|Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take Momotake no Mikoto|lt=Take-ihotake no Mikoto|ja|武五百建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-ihotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi|1=Kamuyaimimi}}."Shinano Ōmiya" in {{Cite book|title=日本の神々 (Japanese Gods)|volume=9|editor=谷川健一|publisher=白水社}}. The shrine received grants of sacred land during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and was recorded as hosting official rites during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era|1=Tenpyō}} (729–749). The presumed site of the {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} government office (''kokufu'') is thought to have been on the Kamikeda Plateau in Ueda City, before moving to present-day Matsumoto City in the early Heian period. The shrine's location near ancient ruins like the {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} suggests it served as a provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} visited by officials."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, the shrine was called "Sōja Ōmiya" or "Shinano Kunitsumigami." During the construction of {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}}, the shrine was designated as its tutelary shrine by the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}, and both the shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}}'s pagoda were maintained with official funds."Shinano Ōmiya Shrine" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|en|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine system and was renamed "Shinano Ōmiya Shrine." == Grounds == [[File:Shinano-omiya-sha keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shrine forest, a designated Natural Monument]] The shrine is surrounded by large trees, forming a sacred grove designated as a Natural Monument by Ueda City. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1860 by {{ill|Matsudaira Tadanari|en|Matsudaira Tadanari|WD=Q11069891|lt=Matsudaira Tadanari|1=Matsudaira Tadanari}}, the lord of Ueda Castle. The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The grounds also feature two auxiliary shrines: ''Rokusho Myōjin'' and ''Komagata Inari Shrine.'' These were relocated during the construction of Ueda Castle in the late {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. File:Shinano-omiya-sha honden.JPG|Main hall (right) and Inari Shrine (left) == Cultural Properties == ; Ueda City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove''' – Designated a Natural Monument on May 9, 1969. == location == ; location * Jōnyū Aza Kamitsunoda 723-1, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}}, or [[:en:Ueda Electric Railway|Ueda Electric Railway]] {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}. ; Nearby sites * {{ill|Ueda Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|信濃国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (ruins) * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-niji|en|信濃国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-niji|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Kokubun-ji|qq=}} (modern successor temple) == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinano-omiya-sha|qq=draft}} * [http://museum.umic.jp/map/document/dot74.html Shinano Ōmiya Shrine Grove] (Ueda City Cultural Property map) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|科野大宮社|version=85679442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:科野大宮社]] {{Commons category|Shinano-omiya-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q17228733}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
